scope: Browser-held encryption and bid-opening key material
Wallet-signed API challenges and bearer sessions
Encrypted invoice envelopes and SQLite persistence
Arc Testnet transaction and metadata exposure
Event projection, RPC dependencies, backups, and operator access
outOfScope: Arc native confidential settlement, which is not currently available
Mainnet funds or production commercial data
Wallet provider, browser extension, and endpoint-device compromise
Security guarantees from unaudited third-party infrastructure

assets.name: Wallet addresses, workflow timing, and revealed bids

trustBoundaries.rule: Only authenticated ciphertext and non-secret workflow context may cross.

trustBoundaries.to: public EVM RPC and Arc Testnet
trustBoundaries.rule: All submitted calldata, transfers, addresses, timing, and revealed bids are public.
Requests require validation, bounded content, rate controls, and wallet authorization.
RPC results are treated as public untrusted input and replayed through a block-hash cursor.
Backups retain encrypted records and sensitive metadata and require restricted storage.

threats.title: Browser key extraction exposes invoice plaintext
controls: Invoice bodies are encrypted in the browser for named recipients.
Plaintext canary tests inspect calldata, logs, contract state, API output, and database projections.
Private keys are not sent to the API.
threats.releaseRequirement: Use an OS-backed or wallet-backed key facility and complete an independent cryptography review before real commercial data.

threats.title: Bid opening is lost, copied, or revealed early
controls: Bid openings are encrypted with wallet-signature-derived AES-GCM keys.
The UI supports encrypted export and import before reveal.
The contract accepts only commitments during the bidding window.
threats.releaseRequirement: Document recovery expectations and independently review wallet-signature key derivation.

threats.title: Authentication challenge replay or bearer-session theft
controls: Challenges are wallet-specific, expire, and are consumed atomically once.
Only SHA-256 session-token hashes are stored.
Sessions expire after 30 minutes.
Authentication endpoints are rate limited per client.
threats.releaseRequirement: Add production transport security, secure browser token storage, revocation controls, and distributed rate limiting before multi-instance operation.

threats.title: Users infer privacy for public chain activity
controls: The website labels current transfers and revealed bids as public.
The payment adapter requires an explicit publicity acknowledgement.
Copy-policy tests reject confidential-settlement claims.
threats.releaseRequirement: Keep public-state notices aligned across README, website, demo, and deployed behavior.

threats.title: Contract authorization or bond-accounting defect
controls: Foundry unit, fuzz, invariant, replay, access-control, and bond-conservation tests run in CI.
Contract deployments pin compiler and optimizer settings.
The Arc Testnet lifecycle smoke gate verifies deployed bytecode and receipts.
threats.releaseRequirement: Complete an independent smart-contract security review and resolve every critical or high finding before real-value use.

threats.title: API or database compromise reveals metadata or active sessions
controls: The API stores ciphertext rather than invoice plaintext.
Envelope access requires a wallet-authenticated party listed in the wrapped recipient keys.
Request bodies are capped at 256 KiB and public errors do not reflect request contents.
Expired challenges and sessions are pruned.
threats.releaseRequirement: Deploy authenticated storage with encryption at rest, access logging, secret management, retention limits, and an incident-response procedure.

threats.title: RPC failure, rate limiting, or reorganization corrupts the read model
controls: Projection requests combine related events in bounded 500-block ranges.
The projector stores a block hash with its cursor and rebuilds on mismatch.
Projection batches apply transactionally and do not overlap.
threats.releaseRequirement: Add provider failover, lag monitoring, and an operator replay command before availability claims.

threats.title: Backup loss or exposure prevents recovery or leaks metadata
controls: SQLite backups use the online backup API for a consistent snapshot.
Restore validates SQLite integrity and refuses to overwrite an existing target.
An automated drill verifies recovered encrypted-envelope and authentication state.
threats.releaseRequirement: Store production backups encrypted, restrict access, test retention, and document service-stop and rollback procedures.

threats.title: Compromised or vulnerable dependency enters the build
controls: The pnpm lockfile is committed and CI installs with frozen versions.
A CycloneDX SBOM records the dependency graph.
Production dependency audit runs as a release check.
threats.releaseRequirement: Review audit findings and SBOM changes for each release and block unresolved critical or high production vulnerabilities.

threats.title: Schema drift or unsafe migration corrupts persisted workflow state
controls: Every schema change advances SQLite user_version in an ordered transaction.
Legacy migration tests preserve projection data while adding required columns.
The API refuses databases created by a newer unsupported schema.
Integrity-checked backup and restore tooling provides a rollback boundary.
threats.releaseRequirement: Back up before every production migration and rehearse forward migration plus rollback against production-sized data.

releaseBlockers: Independent smart-contract and application security review
OS-backed or wallet-backed browser key storage
Production transport security and secret management
Encrypted managed storage and encrypted offsite backups
Incident response, rollback, retention, and access-review procedures
Resolution of all critical and high production dependency findings
